headline: Sarawak Demands Control Over 2,600km Coastline
context: Sarawak submitted formal claim in January 2026 for sovereign management of its 2,600km coastline including fisheries and maritime boundaries.

cards.big: Sarawak claims sovereign control over its 2,600km coastline
cards.sub: Federal maritime agencies currently manage all fisheries and port activities

cards.big: Sarawak fisheries generate RM2.8 billion but state receives only licensing fees
cards.sub: Federal Fisheries Department collects 92% of revenue from Sarawak waters

cards.big: Illegal fishing by 350 foreign vessels detected in Sarawak waters in 2025
cards.sub: State argues federal Maritime Agency lacks resources to patrol extensive coast

cards.big: MA63 legal scholars argue coastal management falls under state jurisdiction
cards.sub: Federal government counters that maritime law is exclusively a federal matter

cards.big: Coastline control tests whether Sarawak sovereignty extends to its own waters
2,600km of coastline jurisdiction reveals that maritime sovereignty is still an unfinished constitutional question.
